did_this_paper_use_ai_to_obtain_results_.details: The paper is a review of methods for early melanoma detection using computer vision and artificial intelligence. It discusses and evaluates various AI methods, including deep learning architectures (VGG-16, VGG-19, MobileNet, Inception v3, DenseNet, Inception-ResNet v2, EfficientNet) and CAD systems for classifying dermatoscopic images of melanoma and other skin lesions. It also mentions the development of a prototype system for analyzing melanocytic images using computer vision and machine learning.

is_this_paper_a_review_paper_or_a_resear.details: The title of the paper is explicitly 'Review of methods for early melanoma detection using computer vision methods' (and 'Обзор методов раннего обнаружения меланомы'). The abstract states that the article discusses existing algorithms, methods of visual diagnosis, and automatic diagnosis systems. It reviews limitations and compares various datasets and neural network architectures (VGG-16, VGG-19, MobileNet, etc.) from prior literature. While it mentions a 'developed prototype system' and its functional diagram, the primary purpose described is summarizing and evaluating existing methods and literature, fitting the definition of a review paper.

non_compliance_reason: The paper is a review, survey, or meta-analysis rather than an original research paper.
